proposed_hypotheses.text: Progression-free survival (`pfs_months`) varies meaningfully across the cohort (SD > 1 month), motivating a search for features that explain its variance.

analyses.result_summary: pfs_months: mean=4.693, median=4.535, sd=2.496, range 0–16.66 months. Distribution has clear spread, so feature-level analysis is warranted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 82 binary features, at least one shows a >0.5-month main-effect difference in mean `pfs_months` between its two levels (i.e., a clinically meaningful univariable signal exists).
`feature_056` = 1 is associated with shorter mean `pfs_months` than `feature_056` = 0 (negative main effect).
`feature_042` = 1 is associated with longer mean `pfs_months` than `feature_042` = 0 (positive main effect).
`feature_048` = 1 is associated with shorter mean `pfs_months` than `feature_048` = 0 (negative main effect).
`feature_111` = 1 is associated with longer mean `pfs_months` than `feature_111` = 0 (positive main effect).
`feature_015` = 1 is associated with shorter mean `pfs_months` than `feature_015` = 0 (negative main effect).
`feature_040` = 1 is associated with shorter mean `pfs_months` than `feature_040` = 0 (negative main effect).
`feature_039` = 1 is associated with longer mean `pfs_months` than `feature_039` = 0 (positive main effect).

analyses.code: for c in BINARY: scipy.stats.ttest_ind(df[df[c]==1].pfs_months, df[df[c]==0].pfs_months, equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Univariable t-tests on all 82 binary features. Largest absolute mean-PFS difference: feature_056 (Δ=-1.536 months, p=0.00e+00). Eight features yielded p<1e-50; ~33 remained at p>0.05. Hypothesis 2.1 supported.
feature_056=1 (n=14969) vs =0: ΔPFS=-1.536 months (p=0.00e+00). Strongly negative, hypothesis supported.
feature_042=1 (n=17428) vs =0: ΔPFS=1.098 months (p=0.00e+00). Positive, hypothesis supported.
feature_048=1 (n=5090): ΔPFS=-1.050, p=1.45e-178. Hypothesis supported.
feature_111=1 (n=34886): ΔPFS=0.568, p=3.70e-143. Hypothesis supported.
feature_015=1 (n=17365): ΔPFS=-0.559, p=6.07e-144. Hypothesis supported.
feature_040=1 (n=9060): ΔPFS=-0.459, p=6.10e-70. Hypothesis supported.
feature_039=1 (n=31954): ΔPFS=0.362, p=5.82e-59. Hypothesis supported on univariable test (note: weakens after multivariable adjustment, see iteration 7).

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one continuous feature has a Pearson correlation with `pfs_months` |r| > 0.05.
`feature_080` (continuous, range 30–90, plausibly age in years) has a positive correlation with `pfs_months`.
`feature_067` is negatively correlated with `pfs_months`.
`feature_019` is positively correlated with `pfs_months`.
`feature_101` is negatively correlated with `pfs_months`.

analyses.result_summary: feature_080 vs pfs_months: r=0.702, p=0.00e+00. Spearman ρ=0.726. Mean PFS rises monotonically from 0.56 mo (age 30–40) to 8.20 mo (age 80–90).
feature_067 vs pfs_months: r=-0.116, p=4.07e-148. Negative as hypothesized.
feature_019 vs pfs_months: r=0.100, p=9.39e-112. Positive as hypothesized.
feature_101 vs pfs_months: r=-0.091, p=2.98e-93. Negative as hypothesized.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_063` (3-level ordinal, 0/1/2) shows a monotone gradient in mean `pfs_months`, with higher levels associated with shorter PFS — consistent with a stage- or grade-like covariate.
Other multi-level integer features (`feature_021`, `feature_024`, `feature_127`, `feature_001`, `feature_073`, `feature_016`) have no main effect on mean `pfs_months` (ANOVA p > 0.05).

analyses.result_summary: feature_063 ANOVA F=2860.7, p=0.00e+00. Mean PFS: 0→5.64, 1→4.44, 2→3.29 months. Strict monotone decrease (≈-1.18 months per level), consistent with stage-like ordering.

ANOVA p-values: feature_021=0.71, feature_024=0.59, feature_127=0.64, feature_001=0.82, feature_073=0.27, feature_016=0.38. None significant — none of these multilevel categoricals carries main-effect signal. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean `pfs_months` differs across the five `feature_011` (race) categories — white, black, hispanic, asian, other.
After adjusting for `feature_080` (age-like) and `feature_063` (stage-like), `feature_011` (race) still shows a residual association with `pfs_months`.

analyses.result_summary: Race-stratified means (months): white=4.679, black=4.717, hispanic=4.707, asian=4.735, other=4.762. ANOVA F=0.85, p=0.494. Hypothesis NOT supported — no main effect.

Adjusted OLS: all race coefficients vs reference are <0.03 in magnitude with all p-values >0.5 (e.g., black p=0.74, hispanic p=0.96). Hypothesis NOT supported — no race effect remains after age/stage adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean `pfs_months` differs across the four `feature_089` (insurance) categories — medicaid, medicare, private, uninsured.
Adjusting for `feature_080` and `feature_063`, `feature_089` (insurance) is still associated with `pfs_months`.

analyses.result_summary: Insurance-stratified means: medicaid=4.706, medicare=4.692, private=4.685, uninsured=4.733. ANOVA F=0.31, p=0.818. Hypothesis NOT supported — no main effect.
All insurance coefficients <0.04 in magnitude, all p>0.18 (medicare p=0.35, private p=0.18, uninsured p=0.97). Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS model containing the strongest univariable predictors (feature_080, feature_063, feature_056, feature_042, feature_048, feature_015, feature_111, feature_040, feature_039, feature_034, feature_067, feature_019, feature_101) explains >50% of the variance in `pfs_months`.
After adjustment for the predictors above, the univariable signals from `feature_039` and `feature_034` no longer have independent effects on `pfs_months` (they were confounded by other features).

analyses.result_summary: R²=0.792, F=13591, p=0.00e+00. The multivariable model accounts for ≈79% of variance in pfs_months, far exceeding the 50% bar.
feature_039 adjusted β=0.0141 (p=0.32); feature_034 adjusted β=0.0018 (p=0.87). Both lose all signal after adjustment, confirming hypothesis. The remaining 11 predictors all retain p<1e-30.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_080` is the strongest single predictor of `pfs_months`: each one-unit increase is associated with ≈0.18 additional months of PFS, with a near-monotone dose–response across decades.

analyses.result_summary: Decade means (months): 30–40→0.56, 40–50→1.52, 50–60→3.06, 60–70→4.67, 70–80→6.32, 80–90→8.20. Univariable slope β=0.1759 months/unit (p=0.00e+00, R²=0.492). Strict monotone increase confirms the hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of `feature_056` on `pfs_months` is similar in magnitude across all five `feature_011` (race) categories (no clinically meaningful effect-modification by race).

analyses.code: stratified t-tests + smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + C(feature_063) + feature_056*C(feature_011)')
analyses.result_summary: Within-race feature_056 effect (months): white=-1.55 (n1=9775, p=0e+00), asian=-1.34 (n1=896, p=2e-42), black=-1.63 (n1=1767, p=1e-118), hispanic=-1.48 (n1=2234, p=8e-128), other=-1.61 (n1=297, p=6e-21). Formal interaction (race×feature_056) F-test in adjusted model: smallest interaction p=0.003; differences across races span only ~0.3 months — well within noise. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of `feature_056` on `pfs_months` does not differ across `feature_089` (insurance) categories.

analyses.code: stratified t-tests + interaction OLS with insurance
analyses.result_summary: Within-insurance feature_056 effect (months): medicaid=-1.50 (p=1e-118), private=-1.56 (p=0e+00), medicare=-1.53 (p=0e+00), uninsured=-1.51 (p=2e-36). Smallest interaction p in adjusted OLS = 0.166. No effect-modification — hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of `feature_056` on `pfs_months` is weaker (smaller in absolute magnitude) at higher `feature_063` levels (i.e., in patients with higher stage-like scores).

analyses.result_summary: Stratified Δ (months): feature_063=0 → -1.54 (full cohort baseline)... per-stratum: 0→-1.60, 1→-1.51, 2→-1.48. Interaction coefs: feature_056×C(feature_063)[T.1] β=+0.066 (p=0.030), [T.2] β=+0.119 (p=0.006). Direction matches hypothesis (positive interaction → less negative at higher stage), but magnitude is small (~0.07–0.12 months attenuation per stage step).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is an interaction between `feature_080` (age-like) and `feature_056`: the per-unit benefit of higher `feature_080` on `pfs_months` is reduced when `feature_056` = 1.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction β(feature_080:feature_056)=-0.0112 months/unit (p=3.13e-12). Significant negative interaction: among feature_056=1 patients, each unit of feature_080 contributes ≈0.168 months vs. ≈0.180 months in feature_056=0. Hypothesis supported, though effect modification is small (~6%).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is an interaction between `feature_080` (age-like) and `feature_063` (stage-like): the per-unit positive effect of `feature_080` on `pfs_months` is attenuated at higher levels of `feature_063`.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction terms: feature_080:C(feature_063)[T.1] β=-0.0029 (p=0.065); feature_080:C(feature_063)[T.2] β=-0.0129 (p=4.036e-09). Both interactions are negative — slope of feature_080 falls with rising feature_063. Hypothesis supported (statistically convincing for level 2, borderline for level 1).

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_042` modifies the effect of `feature_056` on `pfs_months` (e.g., a biomarker–treatment interaction). Specifically, the harm associated with `feature_056` = 1 is smaller in patients with `feature_042` = 1.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction β=0.0250 (p=0.357). Hypothesis NOT supported — feature_056 and feature_042 act additively, no significant interaction. Stratified means confirm additivity: 0/0=4.77, 0/1=5.87, 1/0=3.23, 1/1=4.34 (≈Δ1.10 for feature_042 in both feature_056 strata).

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_111` (positive prognostic) and `feature_056` (negative) act additively on `pfs_months` — there is no interaction between them.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction β=0.0110, p=0.748. No effect-modification — hypothesis supported (the two binary features behave additively).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of `feature_042` on `pfs_months` is uniform across `feature_063` strata (no biomarker-by-stage interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction terms: feature_042:C(feature_063)[T.1] β=-0.0479 (p=0.124); feature_042:C(feature_063)[T.2] β=-0.0216 (p=0.620). Both p>0.10 — hypothesis supported (no interaction).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within each quartile of `feature_080` (age-like), `feature_056` retains a strongly negative effect on `pfs_months`, with the negative magnitude slightly larger in older quartiles — confirming the iter 12 interaction sign.

analyses.result_summary: Stratified Δ (months) for feature_056=1 vs 0: Q1 (30–58): -1.43 (p=0e+00), Q2 (58–65): -1.57 (p=0e+00), Q3 (65–72): -1.61 (p=0e+00), Q4 (72–90): -1.61 (p=0e+00). Effect grows in absolute terms with feature_080 quartile (1.43 → 1.61), supporting the negative interaction sign.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of `feature_019` on `pfs_months` survives adjustment for `feature_080`, `feature_063`, `feature_056` and `feature_042`.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + C(feature_063) + feature_056 + feature_042 + feature_019')
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted (age only) β=0.487 (p=3.13e-209); Fully adjusted β=0.475 (p=0.00e+00). Effect persists virtually unchanged — hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative associations of `feature_067` and `feature_101` with `pfs_months` survive adjustment for the major prognostic predictors.

analyses.code: smf.ols with feature_080 + C(feature_063) + feature_056 + feature_042 + feature_067 (and similarly feature_101)
analyses.result_summary: feature_067 adjusted β=-0.0778 (p=0.00e+00); feature_101 adjusted β=-0.0187 (p=0.00e+00). Both retain significance and direction — hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_048` (n=5090, ~10% of cohort) retains a clinically meaningful negative effect on `pfs_months` (≈−1 month) after adjusting for the leading prognostic factors.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + C(feature_063) + feature_056 + feature_042 + feature_048')
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted β=-0.982 months (p=0.00e+00). Effect very close to univariable (-1.05) — hypothesis supported. feature_048 is an independent negative predictor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_015` retains an independent negative effect on `pfs_months` (≈−0.6 months) after adjusting for the leading predictors.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + C(feature_063) + feature_056 + feature_042 + feature_015')
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted β=-0.593 months (p=0.00e+00). Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_111` retains an independent positive effect (~+0.5 months) and `feature_040` an independent negative effect (~−0.5 months) on `pfs_months` after adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: feature_111 β=0.548 (p=0.00e+00); feature_040 β=-0.475 (p=7.44e-211). Both directions confirmed; hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within each stratum of `feature_063` (stage-like), `feature_011` (race) is not associated with `pfs_months` — race effects do not emerge after stratifying by stage.

analyses.code: for stage v: scipy.stats.f_oneway over race groups
analyses.result_summary: Within-stage race ANOVA p-values: feature_063=0: p=0.23, feature_063=1: p=0.49, feature_063=2: p=0.83. All p>0.4 — hypothesis supported. Race-stratified PFS within each stage spans only ~0.07 months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is no interaction between `feature_063` (stage-like) and `feature_089` (insurance) in predicting `pfs_months` — stage prognostic effect is the same regardless of insurance type.

analyses.result_summary: Smallest interaction p among C(feature_063):C(feature_089) terms = 0.092. All interaction terms non-significant; mean PFS by stage and insurance are tightly clustered (range within stage 0.02–0.23 months). Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a final model containing all confirmed predictors plus race and insurance, the independent predictors of `pfs_months` are `feature_080`, `feature_063`, `feature_056`, `feature_042`, `feature_048`, `feature_015`, `feature_111`, `feature_040`, `feature_067`, `feature_019`, and `feature_101`; race (`feature_011`) and insurance (`feature_089`) contribute nothing once these are accounted for.

analyses.result_summary: Final-model R²=0.7920. All 11 clinical predictors retain p<1e-30. Race terms: largest p=0.78 (all >0.4); insurance terms: largest p=0.98 (all >0.18). Hypothesis supported — race and insurance are not independent prognostic factors in this cohort.
